summary: Pitch‑corrected SBD of the Red Rocks 8/31/78 show delivers strong Jerry work and a solid set, with minor tape pops and an audience patch for missing sections.
review: The 2013 Charlie Miller upgrade offers a crisp soundboard source (with a few audible tape‑static clicks) that has been patched from the audience to fill in the last twenty‑plus minutes.  Musically the performance is praised – first appearances of “Heart Of Me” and “Shakedown,” a fiery “Samson,” and an expansive Terrapin/Playin’/NFA sequence – though some listeners note missing backup vocals on Shakedown and are less fond of the drum jam segment.  Overall the show is regarded as a high‑energy Red Rocks highlight despite the minor technical quirks.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise (especially Jerry’s playing and setlist highlights) balanced against a few recording artifacts and mixed comments on vocals/drum jam yields an overall four‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pitch‑corrected, remastered SBD with occasional tape‑static pops (noted at specific timestamps). Missing ~25 min of the second set were patched from a high‑quality audience recording; overall clarity is solid.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑night Red Rocks set, featuring first performances of “Heart Of Me” and “Shakedown,” a tight mid‑set with Half Step > El Paso, and a sprawling Terrapin/Playin’/NFA suite that keeps the momentum high despite a brief vocal shortfall.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as strong and fast – “Samson, Playin and NFA” showcase his powerful guitar work.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Ollin Arageed drum jam is mentioned but the reviewer dislikes it; overall drumming is solid within the Terrapin sequence.
